tool_args.label: Grand Central Retail Activity 2025 Annual Report & Real Estate Board Materials - March 2026

payload.title: Grand Central Retail Activity 2025 Annual Report / Real Estate information items
payload.description: Two-part board document: (1) Grand Central Terminal Retail Activity 2025 Annual Report covering financial performance, retail portfolio, capital projects, event programs, and marketing; (2) Staff Summary report on agreements including Amendment to Viaduct Agreement with JPMorgan Chase for 270 Park Avenue/Train Shed rehabilitation.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Real estate department of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority, managing Grand Central Terminal retail portfolio and capital projects.
Commuter railroad partnering with MTA Real Estate on Grand Central Terminal operations, retail, and capital projects.
Major Manhattan transit hub and commercial destination operated by Metro-North Railroad with retail, dining, and event spaces.
Bank and developer of 270 Park Avenue headquarters; partner in Train Shed rehabilitation through Viaduct Agreement.
MTA agency responsible for capital construction, party to the Viaduct Agreement with JPMorgan Chase for Train Shed rehabilitation.
Parent authority; party to the Viaduct Agreement and recipient of Board action recommendations.
Retail manager for Grand Central Terminal, collaborating with MTA Retail and Metro-North.
On-call advisory architect for GCT retail architectural review processes.
On-call advisory engineer for GCT retail architectural review processes.
Total gross revenue for Grand Central Terminal in 2025 fiscal year
Projected total gross revenue for Grand Central Terminal in 2026
Total sales increased from $163.3M in 2024 to $196.8M in 2025, a 17% increase

tool_args.label: GCT Gift Shop Q4 Gross Sales 2025
Early Work Agreement - Train Shed Phase 1

payload.description: Authorization to execute an Early Work Agreement (EWA) with JPMC for Phase 1 of Expanded Scope (Train Shed work south of 48th Street). JPMC to pay 100% of costs capped at $50M.
Authorization to negotiate and execute a Sector 2 Amendment for Phase 2 (north of 48th Street). Cost-sharing 50/50 until JPMC contributes $50M. MTA must elect by June 15, 2026.
Sector 2 work has an anticipated duration of 36 months with completion date of May 30, 2029.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Gift Shop Grand Opening

payload.description: Grand Central Gift Shop opened on October 1, 2025 in Graybar Passage, transforming former storage space.

tool_args.label: Grand Central Terminal Train Shed Rehabilitation - Sector 2 / Expanded Scope

payload.project_name: Grand Central Terminal Train Shed Rehabilitation - Sector 2 / Expanded Scope
payload.description: Rehabilitation of the Grand Central Terminal Train Shed underneath Park Avenue. JPMC will manage construction for Expanded Scope (Phase 1 south of 48th St, Phase 2 north of 48th St), contributing $50M toward ~$900M total budget.
payload.location: Park Avenue between 46th and 50th Streets, and side streets, New York, NY

tool_args.label: MNR and MTA Real Estate Collaboration

payload.claim_text: In close partnership, Metro-North and MTA Real Estate advanced new processes to streamline workflows, increase financial transparency, strengthen reporting processes, and reinforce operational accountability.
payload.subject: Metro-North and MTA Real Estate collaboration

payload.claim_text: The strengthened reporting and governance framework reflects a rigor with which the Terminal is managed and underscores our commitment to meeting fiduciary obligations to stakeholders through transparency, accountability, and prudent financial stewardship.
payload.subject: Grand Central Terminal financial governance

payload.claim_text: The structural steel that supports the Train Shed has significantly deteriorated over time and the MTA Parties have embarked on a long-term project to rehabilitate these steel structures to ensure the viability of both the Train Shed and the streets and sidewalks that it supports.
payload.subject: Grand Central Terminal Train Shed structural steel condition
